title: brassica rupestris subsp. rupestris
intro: Brassica rupestris subsp. rupestris, also known as Rock Cress or Alpine Cress, is a plant species belonging to the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its small, white or pink flowers and sharp, peppery taste. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which have been attributed to the presence of glucosinolates and other compounds. Consuming Rock Cress has been associated with several health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as supporting the immune system and preventing certain types of cancer. Rock Cress is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and salves, and is also used as a garnish or ingredient in salads and other dishes due to its distinctive flavor.
